revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Abedani Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Abedani Kofun | native_name =安部谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Abedani Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Abedani Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Abedani Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|28|N|133|06|35|E|region:JP-32|display=inline, title|qq=}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|qid=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|28|N|133|06|35|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Abedani Kofun'''|安部谷古墳||qq=}} is a cluster of five [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo|qq=}} tombs dug in artificial caves, located in the Ōkusa neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|qid=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The cluster of tombs was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202476|title=安部谷古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Abedani Kofun is located in the Ōkusa hills area to the south of the Iu River, which is an area known for its many [[Kofun]]s. The Abedani Kofun cluster has 15 small ''[[kofun]]'', mostly square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||qq=}} or circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||qq=}}, the largest of which has a diameter of 20 meters located on the ridge of a hill, and there are six horizontal corridor-type tombs of the western slope of the hill and four on the eastern slope. These horizontal corridor tombs were excavated in the {{ill|Breccia|en|Breccia|qid=Q191250|lt=Breccia|1=Breccia}} {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} bedrock of the hill and many more are believed to remain undiscovered in the undergrowth. Of these horizontal corridor-type tombs, five lined up in a row at an elevation of 25 meters are covered by the National Historic Site designation. These tombs are numbered from 1 to 5, and none of them have a true passageway, but only a small [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], which is shaped to resemble the stone chambers found in ''[[kofun]]'' Kofuns. Tomb No. 5 is believed have been under construction and never completed as its interior walls are still roughly shaped. Tombs No. 1 to No. 4 have flat burial chambers, with coffin platforms on the left and right sides. Tomb No. 1 is about 2.7 meters wide, about 1.88 meters deep, and about 1.67 meter high, and has a double-chamber structure with a front room and a back room. Tombs No. 2 and No. 3 are single-chambered, and all four tombs have ridges on the boundary lines between the four walls and the ceiling, and relief carvings on the lines of the ridges. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery, cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', fragments of horse harnesses and horse bits. Judging from the style of the Sue pottery, it is believed that these cave tombs were built in the latter half of the 6th century to the first half of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The Abedani Kofun is 3.3 kilometers from {{ill|Higashi-Matsue Station (Shimane)|en|Higashi-Matsue Station (Shimane)|qid=Q5357082|lt=Higashi-Matsue Station|1=Higashi-Matsue Station (Shimane)}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}} and is 800 meters from the {{ill|Izumo Kokufu|en|Izumo Kokufu|qid=Q11395855|lt=Izumo Kokufu|1=Izumo Kokufu}} ruins. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?id=4932&page=1&search_kbn=site&act=&sitename=古天神古墳&sitekana=&target=literature Archaeology Database of Shimane]{{in lang|ja|qq=}} Abedani Kofun
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[en:Abedani Kofun]]
